content.text: A student uploads lecture notes and asks, “Explain these notes like I’m new to the topic, then quiz me with 10 questions.” Claude summarizes, explains, and generates practice questions.
A writer drafts a blog post: “Give me a first draft about using AI safely at work, with 3 sections and a friendly tone,” then iterates with Claude’s suggestions.
A lawyer or consultant drops a 100‑page contract or policy doc and asks for a summary, key risks, and a table of obligations by party; Claude can handle this because of its large context window.
A data analyst uploads CSVs or screenshots of dashboards and asks, “What trend stands out here, and what might explain it?” Claude explains patterns in simple language.
You run Claude Code in your repo and ask, “What does this system do? What are the main services and data flows?” Claude reads the project and returns an architecture overview.
You ask, “Refactor the user authentication module to add multi‑factor auth and improve error handling.” Claude proposes code changes, shows diffs, and can run tests.
Via the API, you build a feature: users paste business emails into your app, and your backend calls Claude to suggest responses or extract structured CRM fields.
Customer support: a bot powered by Claude answers common questions, drafts replies for agents, and flags tricky cases for human review, using cheaper models for most chats and Opus for escalations.

Internal knowledge assistant: connect Claude via MCP connectors to your Slack, Google Drive, and Confluence; employees can ask “What did we decide in last quarter’s planning for project X?” and get links and summaries.
Automation: Managed Agents run nightly—e.g., “Check our CI logs and incident tickets, summarize key issues, and file a report in our incident channel,” using secure access to tools and webhooks.
